title: Product Demand Tracking
description: Activity flow for recording customer demand for out-of-stock or unavailable products and checking demand status

interface.name: DemandService
methods: boolean addProduct(String userId, String prodId, int demandQty)

activity_flows.method: addProduct(String userId, String prodId, int demandQty)
steps: Execute SELECT * FROM user_demand WHERE username=? AND prodid=?
YES → set flag=true (demand already registered), skip insert
NO → Execute INSERT INTO user_demand VALUES(userId, prodId, demandQty)

activity_flows.note: Overloaded convenience method — delegates directly to primary addProduct

steps: Execute SELECT * FROM user_demand WHERE username=? AND prodid=?
YES → Execute DELETE FROM user_demand WHERE username=? AND prodid=?
NO → record not found, set flag=true (idempotent — already removed)
Execute SELECT * FROM user_demand WHERE prodid=?
Create DemandBean(username, prodid, quantity) from row
Return demandList (may be empty if no demand records)

key_decisions: addProduct: demand already registered? → skip duplicate insert vs. insert new record
addProduct: insert row count > 0? → success vs. failure
removeProduct: record exists? → delete vs. skip (both return true — idempotent)
removeProduct: delete row count > 0? → success vs. failure
haveDemanded: loop over all matching rows building demand list
dependencies: DemandServiceImpl uses DBUtil.provideConnection() for DB access
DemandServiceImpl uses DBUtil.closeConnection() for resource cleanup
DemandServiceImpl maps rows to DemandBean objects
addProduct(DemandBean) delegates to addProduct(String, String, int)
error_handling: SQLException caught in all methods; sets flag=false for addProduct/removeProduct, prints stack trace for haveDemanded (returns empty list)
important_notes: user_demand table has composite key (username + prodid). addProduct is idempotent for duplicates (returns true). removeProduct is idempotent for missing records (returns true). haveDemanded is used to find all customers who demanded a product (e.g., to notify them when it is restocked).
